topics.general_information: A headache is pain or discomfort in the head or face area. Many headaches are mild and temporary, but some can be linked to more serious symptoms.
self_care_tips: Rest in a quiet place.
Drink enough fluids.
Avoid bright light if it makes symptoms worse.
Track when the headache started and any other symptoms.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek medical help urgently for a sudden severe headache, headache with confusion, fainting, weakness on one side, chest pain, difficulty breathing, or symptoms that are getting worse.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.

topics.general_information: A fever means the body temperature is higher than usual. It is often a sign that the body is responding to an infection or inflammation.
self_care_tips: Rest and avoid overexertion.
Drink fluids to stay hydrated.
Wear comfortable clothing and avoid overheating.
Monitor symptoms and temperature changes.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek medical help if fever is very high, lasts for several days, occurs with chest pain, shortness of breath, confusion, fainting, stiff neck, or symptoms that rapidly worsen.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.

topics.general_information: High blood pressure means the force of blood against artery walls is higher than recommended over time. It often has no obvious symptoms and is usually measured with a blood pressure device.
self_care_tips: Check blood pressure with a reliable device if available.
Reduce salt intake where appropriate.
Stay physically active within safe limits.
Discuss readings with a healthcare professional.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent medical help if high blood pressure is linked with chest pain, shortness of breath, severe headache, confusion, weakness on one side, or vision changes.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.

topics.general_information: Dehydration happens when the body does not have enough fluids. Mild dehydration can cause thirst, dry mouth, tiredness, darker urine, or dizziness.
self_care_tips: Drink water regularly through the day.
Increase fluids during heat or exercise.
Notice urine color and thirst as simple hydration clues.
Rest if feeling weak or lightheaded.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek medical help if dizziness is severe, fainting occurs, confusion appears, fluids cannot be kept down, or symptoms continue or worsen.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.

topics.general_information: A cough is a reflex that helps clear the airways. It can happen with mild infections, irritation, allergies, or other breathing-related issues.
self_care_tips: Drink fluids to soothe the throat.
Rest and avoid smoke or strong odors.
Use a humid environment if dry air worsens the cough.
Monitor how long the cough lasts and whether new symptoms appear.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek medical help if cough comes with chest pain, shortness of breath, blue lips, coughing blood, high fever, or symptoms that worsen or last a long time.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.

topics.general_information: Chest pain can have many causes, some minor and some serious. Because it can be linked to urgent conditions, it should be treated carefully.
self_care_tips: Stop strenuous activity and rest.
Notice whether pain is severe, sudden, spreading, or linked with breathing difficulty.
Do not ignore chest pain that feels unusual or intense.
Seek professional guidance if unsure.
topics.when_to_seek_help: Seek urgent medical help immediately for severe chest pain, chest pain with shortness of breath, dizziness, fainting, sweating, confusion, or pain spreading to the arm, back, neck, or jaw.
topics.disclaimer: This is general information only and is not a diagnosis or treatment plan. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for personal medical advice.
